explanation: Citrus aurantium bergamia (bergamot) fruit extract is a botanical antioxidant rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that protect skin cells from oxidative damage. It contains bergapten, a furanocoumarin that can cause photosensitivity reactions on reactive or fair skin — look for bergapten-free (FCF) versions in leave-on products.

Urtica dioica (stinging nettle) extract is a plant-derived antioxidant with mild anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, used to calm redness and reduce surface irritation. The plant's sting comes from formic acid and histamine that are neutralized during extraction, making the finished extract generally well-tolerated by sensitive skin.

Eschscholtzia californica (California poppy) leaf extract is a botanical antioxidant containing alkaloids and flavonoids with documented anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It is considered well-tolerated in topical formulas and is used primarily to calm reactive or stressed skin.

1,2-Hexanediol is a synthetic diol that functions as both a mild preservative booster and humectant in skincare formulas, helping to prevent microbial growth while drawing moisture to the skin. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is widely used as a gentler alternative to traditional preservatives like parabens and isothiazolinones.

10-Decanediol is a long-chain diol used as a preservative booster and antimicrobial agent in skincare formulas, often combined with other mild preservatives for broad-spectrum protection. It is considered well-tolerated and does not have the sensitization concerns associated with more aggressive preservatives like methylisothiazolinone.

10-Hydroxydecanoic acid is a naturally occurring medium-chain hydroxy fatty acid found in royal jelly with documented barrier-supporting and mild antimicrobial properties. It helps reinforce the skin's lipid barrier and is well-tolerated including by sensitive and reactive skin types.

2-Hexanediol is a diol solvent and humectant that helps skin retain moisture while also functioning as a mild preservative booster in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated and is considered a low-sensitization alternative to more aggressive humectant glycols like propylene glycol.

2-Hexanediol is a diol solvent and humectant that helps skin retain moisture while also functioning as a mild preservative booster in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated and is considered a low-sensitization alternative to more aggressive humectant glycols like propylene glycol.

3-Butanediol is a short-chain diol used as a humectant and solvent in skincare formulas, helping to draw moisture to the skin surface and improve the absorption of other ingredients. It has a low sensitization profile comparable to butylene glycol and is suitable for sensitive skin.

3-O-Ethyl ascorbic acid is a stable, ether-bonded vitamin C derivative that penetrates skin more effectively than water-soluble forms, converting to active ascorbic acid in skin cells to deliver antioxidant protection and inhibit melanin production. It is significantly more stable than pure L-ascorbic acid and causes considerably less irritation, making it a practical brightening active for reactive or sensitive skin.

4-Terpineol is a naturally occurring terpene alcohol found in tea tree oil and pine, used in skincare as a fragrance component with mild antimicrobial properties. While less irritating than many other terpenes, it can cause contact sensitization in fragrance-reactive individuals with repeated exposure.

6-Naphthalate is a synthetic ester used as a lightweight emollient in skincare formulas, providing a smooth, non-greasy skin feel and helping to soften and condition the skin surface. It is generally well-tolerated across skin types and is commonly used as a texture enhancer in moisturizers and serums.

Acer saccharum (sugar maple) extract is a botanical antioxidant derived from maple sap, containing polyphenols and gluconic acid that provide mild exfoliating and free-radical-scavenging benefits. It is considered well-tolerated and is used in formulas targeting skin tone evenness and environmental protection.

Acetyl Hexapeptide-8, known commercially as Argireline, is a peptide that inhibits neurotransmitter release at the neuromuscular junction to temporarily relax the facial muscle contractions that cause dynamic expression lines. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and non-irritating at typical use concentrations of 3–10%.

Acetyl Tetrapeptide-15 is a synthetic peptide designed to reduce skin's perception of stinging and discomfort by modulating the substance P pain signaling pathway, making it particularly useful in formulas for reactive or sensitized skin. It is generally well-tolerated and used in post-procedure and calming products to reduce the sensation of redness and irritation.

Acetylated glycol stearate is a synthetic wax ester derived from stearic acid and ethylene glycol, used as an emollient and emulsifier that gives moisturizers and lotions a smooth, creamy texture. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and does not typically clog pores or sensitize at standard formula concentrations.

Acrylates copolymer is a synthetic polymer used as a thickener, film-former, and stabilizer in gels, serums, and emulsions, giving them their characteristic texture and consistency. It can form a surface film that occasionally contributes to pilling when layered with incompatible formulas, but is considered non-sensitizing and well-tolerated by sensitive skin.

Acrylates/vinyl isodecanoate crosspolymer is a crosslinked synthetic polymer used as a thickener and texture modifier in skincare formulas, providing a smooth, lightweight, non-tacky skin feel. It is considered non-sensitizing and well-tolerated, with low occlusive potential suitable for oily and combination skin types.

Acrylates/ammonium methacrylate copolymer is a synthetic polymer used as a thickener and suspension agent in skincare formulas, helping to stabilize emulsions and deliver a light, non-greasy texture. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing across skin types.

Acrylates/C10-30 alkyl acrylate crosspolymer is one of the most widely used synthetic polymer thickeners in skincare, responsible for the gel-like texture in many serums, sunscreens, and moisturizers. It is non-sensitizing, has no significant comedogenic potential, and is well-tolerated across all skin types including reactive and sensitive.

Acrylates/dimethicone copolymer is a hybrid silicone-acrylate polymer that provides a smooth, silky skin feel while functioning as a light film-former and texture enhancer. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and tends to pill less than standard dimethicone when layered over water-based formulas.

Acrylates/methoxy PEG-15 methacrylate copolymer is a PEG-modified synthetic polymer used as a thickener and emulsifier, contributing a smooth, lightweight texture that spreads evenly on skin. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol component.

Acrylates/octylacrylamide copolymer is a film-forming polymer primarily used in sunscreens and long-wear formulas to improve water resistance and product longevity on skin. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and is considered non-sensitizing at typical use concentrations.

Acrylates/polytrimethylsiloxymethacrylate copolymer is a silicone-modified acrylate polymer used as a film-former and sensory modifier in long-wear and water-resistant skincare and makeup formulas. It is generally well-tolerated and provides a smooth finish without significant occlusive or comedogenic potential.

Acrylates/stearyl acrylate/dimethicone methacrylate copolymer is a silicone-hybrid polymer used as a film-former and sensory modifier in skincare and makeup formulas, providing a smooth, non-greasy finish. It offers improved compatibility with water-based layers compared to standard dimethicone and is well-tolerated by sensitive skin.

Acrylonitrile appears in cosmetic ingredient lists only as a trace residual monomer within acrylonitrile-derived film-forming polymers, not as a standalone ingredient — the pure monomer is an industrial chemical regulated to minimal residual levels in finished cosmetic products. At trace concentrations in formulas, risk is considered low, but it is a recognized carcinogen in industrial form and its presence reflects a manufacturing residual rather than an intentional functional ingredient.

Acrylonitrile/methyl methacrylate/vinylidene chloride copolymer is a hollow microsphere polymer used as a texture-enhancing filler in skincare and makeup formulas, creating a lightweight, soft-focus effect on the skin surface. The copolymer itself is considered inert and well-tolerated, with residual monomers regulated to safe levels in finished cosmetic products.

Actinidia chinensis (kiwi) fruit extract is a botanical antioxidant rich in vitamin C precursors, vitamin E, and polyphenols that protect skin cells from oxidative stress and support a more even skin tone. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and is used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant and mild tone-evening properties.

Actinidia chinensis fruit extract is a kiwi-derived botanical antioxidant containing vitamin C precursors and polyphenols that help neutralize free radicals and support skin brightness. It is generally well-tolerated and suitable for sensitive skin at typical formula concentrations.

Adansonia digitata (baobab) seed oil is a rich emollient pressed from the seeds of the African baobab tree, high in oleic, linoleic, and palmitic fatty acids that support barrier repair and skin suppleness. It absorbs relatively quickly for an oil, has low comedogenic potential, and is well-suited for dry, mature, and combination skin types.
